_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_PLAYER: === HOW TO CREATE OR UPDATE YOUR CHARACTERS ===
1. Open Gemini (gemini.google.com) or any AI chat
2. Upload or paste this entire file
3. Tell the AI what characters you want, for example:
'Fill in these 3 characters for Hoard of the Dragon Queen:
PC1: A human fighter soldier who uses a greatsword
PC2: A half-elf bard entertainer who talks her way out of everything
PC3: A high elf wizard sage obsessed with ancient dragon lore'
4. The AI will fill in all the stats, spells, equipment, and features
5. Copy the JSON output the AI gives you (everything between the curly braces)
=== HOW TO IMPORT ===
FULL PARTY (all characters at once):
- In the app: go to Systems tab > Dev > Paste JSON area
- Paste the full JSON with the pcs array and tap Import
SINGLE CHARACTER UPDATE:
- Open the character's sheet > tap Edit
- Tap the '📋 Update from JSON' button at the top
- Paste just that one character's JSON (no pcs wrapper needed)
- Check 'Preserve HP, XP, conditions & inventory' to keep live game data
- Tap Apply
TIPS:
- You can ask for ANY race/class combo from D&D 5e
- Ask the AI to roll stats or use point buy — your call
- If something looks wrong, ask the AI to fix it before importing
- The AI should NOT include the _INSTRUCTIONS fields in its output
- If you only want to update 1 or 2 characters, that's fine — the app matches by id
- For single-character updates, you can paste just the character object (no pcs wrapper)
_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_AI: INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE AI FILLING THIS OUT:
Fill in 3 Level 1 D&D 5e characters for Hoard of the Dragon Queen.
Use the EXACT JSON structure below — do not add or remove any fields.
Replace all placeholder values with real D&D 5e data.
RULES:
- Keep id fields exactly as pc1, pc2, pc3
- Keep color fields as-is (#c04a3a, #4a7090, #7060a0)
- Stats format: '16 (+3)' — always include the modifier in parens
- HP must match class hit die + CON modifier (Fighter d10, Wizard d6, Bard d8, etc.)
- AC must reflect actual armor worn (leather=11+DEX, chain mail=16, etc.)
- initiative = DEX modifier number only (e.g. 2 for DEX 14)
- passive_perception = 10 + WIS modifier + proficiency if proficient in Perception
- passive_insight = 10 + WIS modifier + proficiency if proficient in Insight
- skills: list ALL proficient skills with correct modifiers, e.g. 'Athletics +5, Perception +3'
- skillProfs: array of proficient saving throws AND skill names, e.g. ['STR Save', 'CON Save', 'Athletics', 'Perception']. Include the two class saving throw proficiencies plus all proficient skills.
- features: ALL class + racial features. You can use EITHER format:
String format: 'FEATURE NAME: Description.\nFEATURE2: Description.' (separated by \n)
Array format: [{'name': 'Feature Name', 'description': 'Full description'}] — the app auto-converts this
- magic: empty string for non-casters. For casters use: 'Spellcasting: [Ability] | Save DC: [N] | Attack Bonus: +[N]\nCantrips: ...\nLevel 1 Spells: ...'
- slots: [] for non-casters. For casters: [{'max': 2, 'used': 0}] for each spell level (index 0 = 1st level, index 1 = 2nd level, etc.)
- spellbook: [] for non-casters. For casters: fill with spell objects using the format shown in the template. Include ALL known/prepared spells with full descriptions.
- attacks: structured attack entries for weapons and combat cantrips. Include at least all equipped weapons.
- resources: trackable per-rest abilities like Second Wind, Bardic Inspiration, etc. Format: {'name':'...','max':N,'used':0,'restore':'short' or 'long' or 'dawn','desc':'...'}
- inventory: starting equipment with weights. type = weapon/armor/shield/tool/supply/potion/scroll/consumable/loot/misc/key. Include notes with damage dice, properties
- familiar: null for characters without a familiar. For characters with one (e.g. Find Familiar), use the familiar object format shown in the template.
- languages: array of known languages, e.g. ['Common', 'Dwarvish']
- backstory fields: Fill in origin, motivation, secret, personality, ideals, bonds, flaws with 1-3 sentences each based on the character concept
OUTPUT: Return ONLY the JSON with the pcs array (no _INSTRUCTIONS fields). No markdown code fences, no commentary — just raw JSON.
The player will paste your output into their D&D campaign app.

pcs.name: CHARACTER NAME
pcs.race: Race (e.g. Human, Dwarf, Elf, Tiefling, Dragonborn)
pcs.skills: List all proficient skills with modifiers, e.g. Athletics +5, Perception +3, Stealth +4
pcs.features:
ALL CLASS AND RACIAL FEATURES in caps with descriptions, separated by \n. Example:
FIGHTING STYLE (Defense): +1 AC while wearing armor.
SECOND WIND (Bonus Action, 1/Short Rest): Regain 1d10+1 HP.
pcs.magic:
Leave empty for non-casters. For casters use format:
Spellcasting: [Ability] | Save DC: [N] | Attack Bonus: +[N]
Cantrips: Spell1, Spell2
Level 1 Spells: Spell1, Spell2, Spell3

spellbook._comment: Include one entry per known/prepared spell. Remove this example for non-casters.
spellbook.name: Spell Name
spellbook.components: V, S, M (a bit of fleece)

_familiar_format._comment: Use this format if the character has a familiar (e.g. Find Familiar). Replace familiar: null with this object.
_familiar_format.name: Familiar Name
_familiar_format.notes: Flyby, Keen Hearing & Sight, darkvision 120 ft.

pcs.backstory_origin: Where the character comes from and how they grew up
pcs.bonds: Who or what they care about
